links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The streamer-tailed tyrant (Gubernetes yetapa) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, and Paraguay, and as a vagrant to Uruguay.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | last2=Rumboll |first2=Maurice| title =Birds of Southern South America and Antarctica | publisher =Princeton University Press | series =Princeton Illustrated Checklists | date =2001 | location =New Jersey | pages =Plate 77, map 77.14 | isbn =0-691-09035-1 }} It has also been documented as a vagrant in Uruguay. The streamer-tailed tyrant inhabits wet grasslands and marshy areas with nearby streams and shrubby areas, and also groves of moriche palm
